summary: Pester tests provide solid unit-level coverage of core module functions (parsing, aggregation, flaky detection, markdown formatting) and thorough workflow structure validation (triggers, jobs, dependencies, actionlint). However, significant gaps exist: the CLI entry point (Aggregate-TestResults.ps1) is untested, environment variables ($GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY), parameters (-SummaryPath, -AllowFailures, -InputDirectory), and machine-greppable output lines (AGGREGATE_*=) lack Pester coverage. Edge cases are limited—no duration precision/rounding tests, no large test counts, no special character escaping validation, no markdown syntax verification. Error handling is good (invalid XML/JSON, missing files, malformed data), but Find-FlakyTests and Get-AggregateTotals have minimal test cases. Tests are well-organized with clear names and good fixture isolation via temporary files. The separate Run-ActTests.ps1 harness provides integration testing but is not part of the Pester suite as required. Overall, the tests would catch regressions in core parsing/aggregation logic and workflow structure, but would miss CLI bugs, parameter handling issues, and output formatting errors.
